name: Lower Chicksgrove
overview: Lower Chicksgrove is a small hamlet on the north bank of the River Nadder in the Vale of Wardour, in the civil parish of Sutton Mandeville, about 2 miles east of Tisbury and roughly 11 miles west of Salisbury. Recorded as Chicksgrove in the 12th century, it is a scattered cluster of farmsteads and cottages, many built of local Chilmark stone. Chicksgrove Manor, the largest surviving house, was the birthplace of the Elizabethan poet and lawyer Sir John Davies (1569-1626).
character: A tranquil, picturesque Nadder-valley hamlet of stone and thatched cottages along quiet lanes, deep in unspoilt countryside.
amenities: The Compasses Inn - a historic 14th-century country pub with restaurant and rooms
No shop or school in the hamlet - nearest services are in Tisbury (~2 miles)
Tisbury has the nearest railway station, shops and schools

commutes.notes: About 2 miles for the station, shops and schools
About 11 miles east via the Nadder valley and A30
Direct from Tisbury (about 2 miles) on the Salisbury-Exeter line (South Western Railway)

prices.summary: Stock is mainly period stone and thatched cottages and houses in a Vale of Wardour setting within the Cranborne Chase National Landscape, in very low volumes; character and tranquillity support premium values. See the live Rightmove sold-price page for current figures.

thingsToDo: Walking the Nadder valley and Vale of Wardour (a popular circular walk starts from the Compasses Inn)
Visiting Old Wardour Castle and the small town of Tisbury nearby
placesToEat: The Compasses Inn, Lower Chicksgrove - award-winning country pub and restaurant with rooms
pros: Exceptionally tranquil, picturesque stone-built Nadder-valley hamlet
A renowned historic country pub with rooms in the hamlet itself
Direct rail to London and Salisbury from Tisbury, about 2 miles away
cons: No shop or school in the hamlet - everyday needs are met in Tisbury
Very rural and car-dependent, on narrow lanes
London trains from Tisbury are slower (about 2 hours) than from Salisbury
whoItSuits: Buyers seeking a deeply rural, characterful Nadder-valley hamlet with a great country pub and a station at Tisbury close by.

sources.label: Rural View - Little Cottage, Lower Chicksgrove (local Chilmark stone, setting)
